title: elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. ferrugineus
intro: Elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. ferrugineus, also known as the rusty-leaved blue berry ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the immune system and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. ferrugineus has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and skin infections, due to its antipyretic, expectorant, and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are commonly used to promote overall health and well-being.
